title: Investigation

hero.subtitle: ML-generated investigation leads from anomaly detection

description: Each case represents a cluster of suspicious contracts identified through statistical anomaly detection. Review, corroborate, and validate against external sources.

sections.confirmedSubtitle: Externally validated through news reports, ASF audits, and legal proceedings

empty: No cases match the current filters.

funnel.hitRateText: {{confirmed}} of {{checked}} investigated vendors have documented corruption investigations

groundTruth.title: Ground Truth
groundTruth.subtitle: Documented corruption cases in Mexican government procurement

workflowGuide.title: How to Use This Workflow

step1.label: Review
step1.desc: Read the case summary and examine the linked contracts. Check the risk score distribution.

step2.label: Corroborate
step2.desc: You found external evidence (news article, ASF audit report, court record, or SAT data) confirming the suspicious pattern.

step3.label: Refute
step3.desc: You found a legitimate explanation — emergency contract exemption, sole qualified supplier, or a prior audit cleared this vendor.

step4.label: Inconclusive
step4.desc: The pattern is flagged but you found no external evidence yet. Leave for monitoring or pass to another investigator.

workflowGuide.groundTruthNote: Corroborated cases can be submitted as new ground truth for v6.5 model retraining, improving future detection.

queue.empty: No cases match the current filters.

caseDetail.noEvidence: No evidence recorded yet. Add external sources to support or refute this case.
caseDetail.loadError: Could not load case. It may not exist.

promote.confirm: This will add the case to the ground truth database and use it to retrain the risk model in the next cycle. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
promote.success: Case promoted to ground truth successfully.

noCasesYet: No cases have been created yet

intake.subtitle: What do you want to investigate?

search.placeholder: Search by vendor, institution, sector, fraud type...

headerDesc: Algorithmically generated investigations. Each case represents a statistical risk pattern that requires journalistic corroboration.
headerPipeline: In progress: automated detection → investigation → corroboration → definitive archive

asfLookup.title: ASF Audit Matches
asfLookup.subtitle: Findings from the Auditoría Superior de la Federación matching vendors in this case
asfLookup.noMatches: No ASF findings found for vendors in this case.

verify.heading: How to verify this lead
verify.step1: Search COMPRANET (compranet.hacienda.gob.mx) for this vendor's RFC or full name to access original contract records
verify.step2: Cross-reference against SAT EFOS list (sat.gob.mx) to check if the vendor is classified as a ghost company
verify.step3: Review ASF audit reports (asf.gob.mx) for the contracting institution in the relevant year
verify.step4: Request documents via Plataforma Nacional de Transparencia (infomex.gob.mx) under articles 70-73 of LGTAIP
verify.editorial: Suggested citation: 'According to RUBLI procurement analysis, contracts awarded to [vendor] show procurement patterns statistically similar to documented corruption cases (risk score: [X]/1.0). This does not constitute proof of wrongdoing and requires journalistic corroboration.'
verify.disclaimer: Risk scores are statistical indicators, not legal determinations. High scores indicate similarity to documented corruption patterns and require independent verification before publication.

riskScoreTooltip: Risk score = statistical similarity to known corruption patterns. Not proof of wrongdoing.
loadingError: Could not load information. Please try again later.

methodology.body: Each case represents a cluster of statistically anomalous contracts, detected by the PyOD ensemble (IForest + COPOD) and confirmed by v6.5 risk score > 0.60. "Corroborated" cases have been verified against external sources (ASF, SFP, investigative journalism). "Pending" cases are active leads without external verification yet.
